task_completion.description: Verify the required tags are created in the Crimson 3.0 project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Navigation Pane under 'Data Tags'. You should see 'Raw_Level' (Float), 'Filtered_Level' (Float), 'Level_Array' (Float Array, Extent 10), and 'Filter_Index' (Integer).

task_completion.description: Verify a User Program named 'SMA_Filter' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Navigation Pane under 'Programs'. A program named 'SMA_Filter' should be listed and selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'SMA_Filter' program is configured for Background 1 execution with a 100 ms tick.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With 'SMA_Filter' selected, look at the properties/configuration pane. The 'Task' or 'Execution' setting should be set to 'Background 1' and the 'Tick' or 'Rate' should be set to '100 ms'.

task_completion.description: Verify the SMA filter logic is written in the program editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code editor pane for 'SMA_Filter'. It should contain C-like syntax referencing 'Raw_Level', 'Level_Array', 'Filter_Index', and 'Filtered_Level' to calculate a moving average.

task_completion.description: Verify the script code is saved to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like Notepad) open with the SMA logic, showing the filename 'sma_filter.txt' in the title bar, or a File Explorer window showing 'sma_filter.txt' inside 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\CrimsonTasks'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Crimson project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the Crimson 3.0 application. It should display 'chemical_mixer_filtered.c3', or look for a File Explorer window showing the file in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Crimson 3.0 GUI to create the project and tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Red Lion Crimson 3.0 application interface being interacted with, rather than just a text editor modifying a raw .c3 file.

integrity.description: Verify the script logic actually implements a moving average.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in 'SMA_Filter' should show array manipulation (e.g., shifting values or using an index) and a sum/division operation, rather than just assigning 'Raw_Level' directly to 'Filtered_Level'.

integrity.description: Verify the files are saved in the correct directories, not just named correctly in a temporary location.
integrity.visual_evidence: File Explorer paths or 'Save As' dialogs should explicitly show 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\CrimsonTasks' and 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create a Crimson 3.0 project saved as 'chemical_mixer_filtered.c3' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\'. The project must contain tags: 'Raw_Level' (Float), 'Filtered_Level' (Float), 'Level_Array' (Float Array, Extent: 10), and 'Filter_Index' (Integer). A User Program named 'SMA_Filter' must be created, configured for 'Background 1' execution with a 100 ms tick, and contain the SMA logic. The script code must also be saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\CrimsonTasks\sma_filter.txt'.
